brand.name: NexGen Yachting
brand.tagline: Own a Luxurious Private Yacht at a Fraction of the Cost

offer.what: Fractional co-ownership of luxury sailing catamarans (1/6 shares, up to 5 weeks/year; 1/4 shares up to 8 weeks/year). Fleet of 8+ catamarans (55–100 ft) rotating Mediterranean summers / Caribbean winters. Full management, crew, and charter brokerage included. Separate yacht charter service also offered.
offer.coreOutcome: Live the private-yacht lifestyle — Mediterranean and Caribbean sailings with crew — without the full cost, management burden, or idle-boat waste of sole ownership, while charter income offsets or covers mortgage payments.
offer.pricePoint: $250K–$4.25M per 1/6 share depending on vessel; financing up to 80% LTV available (20% down minimum, ~$50K entry). Example: Lagoon 65 at $575K/share; Bali 5.2 at $250K/share; Sunreef 100 Power at $4.25M/share for 1/4.
offer.commitment: Long-term co-ownership contract; recurring management fee (amount not publicly disclosed); annual usage windows scheduled in advance.
leadMagnets: Glossy ownership brochure (request via contact form)
Email subscription for new inventory / yacht updates

audience.icp: Affluent US-based individuals (HNW, $500K+ net worth) who love sailing/yachting, travel the Mediterranean and Caribbean, and want the lifestyle without the full $3M–$10M outlay or year-round operational headaches. Often 40–65, business owners, executives, or retirees.
painPoints: Full yacht ownership is prohibitively expensive (purchase + crew + maintenance + marina)
A personally-owned yacht sits idle 47+ weeks per year — capital destruction
Managing a yacht remotely is operationally overwhelming
Chartering feels like renting rather than owning; no personalization or investment upside
Fear that fractional/co-ownership schemes are gimmicks or financially risky
segments: Lifestyle buyers: want the experience and bragging rights, ROI secondary
Investment-minded buyers: focused on charter income covering carrying costs
First-time yacht aspirants: dream of owning but never thought it was possible
Current charter clients: already booking weeks, want equity instead of rental spend

salesCycle.length: Weeks to months — high-ticket, complex due diligence; typically 4–12 weeks from first contact to signed agreement
salesCycle.trial: No trial; free consultation / brochure download as entry point. Prospects can charter before committing to ownership (separate charter service acts as a soft trial).
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Inbound inquiry (web form / phone) → sales call → custom proposal with vessel options → contract signing. Fully sales-assisted; no self-serve checkout.
salesCycle.topObjection: Trust / legitimacy: 'Is this a real investment or a gimmick?' — mixed online reviews and niche category mean skepticism is high. Secondary: 'Will I actually get the weeks I want?' (scheduling conflicts among co-owners).

ahaMoment: The first time the new co-owner steps aboard their yacht for their first ownership week — validated by a seamless booking experience and professional crew. Pre-purchase proxy aha: receiving the personalized brochure or seeing their chosen vessel's specs and charter income projections for the first time.

retention.recurring: Annual management fee; seasonal booking cycle (Mediterranean summer / Caribbean winter) locks owners into recurring engagement. Charter income distribution keeps them financially connected.
retention.expansion: Upgrading to a larger or newer vessel; purchasing a second share on a different yacht; referring family or friends who co-own alongside them.
retention.churnRisk: Charter income underperforms projections; scheduling friction with co-owners; yacht condition or crew quality disappointments; competitor or resale exit if resale market develops.

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding: no evident automated post-purchase sequence to orient new co-owners on booking process, crew contacts, and what to expect on board
Lead-Magnet Nurture: brochure downloaders and inventory subscribers likely receive no structured nurture — cold leads go dark after initial interest
Trial/Consult Conversion: phone/form inquiries likely drop off after initial sales call with no follow-up sequence to re-engage ghosted prospects
Value Newsletter: no visible content cadence educating leads/owners on destinations, market trends, charter ROI, or lifestyle content to maintain top-of-mind
Renewal & Expansion: no visible mechanism to prompt existing co-owners to upgrade vessel, add a second share, or refer peers
Win-Back / Re-Engagement: no evident campaign for leads who inquired, downloaded the brochure, but never booked a call or signed
toneVoice: Aspirational yet accessible — luxury without aloofness. Confident and sophisticated but conversationally warm ('Cheers to you'). Speaks to the dream first, then grounds it in financial logic. Avoid stuffy nautical jargon; write like a knowledgeable friend who owns a yacht and wants to share the secret.
compliance: Standard CAN-SPAM / GDPR compliance required. Investment return claims (charter income, cash-on-cash returns) must be framed carefully — use 'projected,' 'historically,' or 'up to' language and include appropriate disclaimers. No HIPAA or specific financial-services licensing constraints identified, but return projections require careful copy review.
